diagnosis.final_diagnosis: Poorly Differentiated Sarcoma with Metastases to Lung, Rectum, Bladder, Prostate
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The diagnosis was supported by imaging findings of a bulky rectal mass with features suggesting a mucinous neoplasm, and pathology from core needle biopsies demonstrating a very poorly differentiated malignant neoplasm with chondroid differentiation. The tumor was positive for vimentin and focally positive for S100, indicating a high-grade sarcoma, likely a malignant peripheral nerve sheath tumor, with the pathologist unable to entirely exclude melanoma.
